reasoning: VIX at 17.94 (MID regime, flat direction, +0.45% overnight) signals neutral IV environment — neither suppressive nor volatile. Overnight gap +$1.75 up is moderately bullish, with premarket recovery to 744.07 (near 744.70 high) confirming conviction. Ribbon in BULL stack with 54¢ spread provides bullish structure. Two HIGH severity events (CPI + Retail Sales) fire simultaneously at 08:30 ET premarket — no RTH no-trade block after 09:00 ET, as both are absorbed 90+ minutes before 09:35 entry window. Retail sales already printed (+0.5% MoM); CPI is known-risk within the 08:30 window only. Macro backdrop is risk-on with no intraday announcements blocking RTH. Long bias supported into open.
key_observations: VIX 17.94 mid-regime, flat direction: no fear, no complacency. Neutral volatility signal.
Overnight gap +$1.75 up (0.24% of SPY 742.32 prior close): moderately bullish sentiment from overnight session. Sets opening bias toward long.
CPI + Retail Sales both release 08:30 ET premarket. Combined no-trade window ends 09:00 ET. News will be 35 minutes old by 09:35 entry gate — tape fully absorbed by open.
Ribbon in BULL stack with 54-cent spread (normal). Premarket high 744.7, low 742.31 — constructive range, no extreme whipsaw.

Sector internals mixed: XLK -0.24%, XLF -0.29% (slight defensive), XLE +0.28% (energy stable). SPY itself +0.52% overnight. Rotation signal = mixed, consistent with early-session positioning before CPI print.
